ctx.name: Carl
ctx.notes: Carl is finding it hard to deal with CoE and AZP app moving between two very diffenent ptojects.

introQueue.label: Outside of work
introQueue.name: What's been the best part of your world outside of work lately?
introQueue.description: Explicitly non-work. Kim Scott's 'care personally' — shows genuine interest in the whole person.

introQueue.label: Agenda check
introQueue.name: Before we get into it, anything you want to make sure we cover today?
introQueue.description: Semi-set early question. Gives the report explicit permission to set the agenda before the manager's plan takes over.

introQueue.label: Friction scan
introQueue.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
introQueue.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

introQueue.label: Opening pace check
introQueue.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
introQueue.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

focus_points.label: CoE vs AZP context switching friction
focus_points.reason: Carl is finding it hard to deal with CoE and AZP app moving between two very different projects. One possibility is that the switching cost is eating focus time, but it could also be unclear ownership boundaries or competing priorities—worth clarifying what part feels hardest right now.

focus_points.label: Which project gets his best attention
focus_points.reason: Whether the sequence of his top priorities is still coherent across the two streams—so he isn’t implicitly overcommitting to both at once.

focus_points.label: Workload split across two project streams
focus_points.reason: Whether his capacity is being stretched by the overlap between CoE and AZP work, and whether the current split is sustainable for the next two weeks.

brief.coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in is likely about how Carl, as a UX Lead, can manage switching costs and competing demands across CoE and AZP.
brief.openingQuestion: What part of moving between CoE and AZP is costing you the most focus right now: priorities, ownership, or the actual context switching?
listenFor: whether he names one project as repeatedly interrupting the other
whether he can point to a specific decision, meeting, or stakeholder causing the friction
whether he hesitates to rank CoE versus AZP for the next two weeks
avoid: do not treat this as a personal resilience problem before testing priority and ownership clarity
do not ask him to keep both projects equally moving if the tradeoffs are not explicit
brief.goodOutcome: You and Carl have agreed which project gets lead-level priority for the next two weeks and what ownership boundary or tradeoff that implies across the other stream.
brief.suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where Carl had to switch between CoE and AZP so you can ground the conversation in a real tradeoff.
